title: Shohei Ohtani announces birth of second child, takes paternity leave
summary: The Los Angeles Dodgers baseball player announced with his wife the birth of their second child, so he missed a game to take paternity leave.

title: Japan's emperor and empress arrive in Belgium for first official visit in 27 years
summary: Emperor Naruhito and Empress Masako were received by Princess Elisabeth of Belgium at the airport after concluding their schedule in the Netherlands, as part of their European tour.

title: Japan faces Tunisia in search of first World Cup win
summary: Japan plays its second World Cup match against Tunisia, with the mission of overcoming the opposing defense and breaking the adverse trend in second group stage matches.

title: Mutilated remains of a man found in a freezer in a Kobe building
summary: Police are investigating the discovery of a male body with clear signs of mutilation stored in a freezer inside an apartment in central Kobe.

title: Yomiuri Giants win thanks to Asano's home run and Witly's performance
summary: Rookie Asano Shōgo hit a decisive home run and pitcher Witly dominated for eight no-hit innings, leading the Giants to victory.

title: Israel continues attacks in Lebanon despite ceasefire with Hezbollah
summary: Despite the ceasefire agreement, the Israeli army carried out bombings in southern Lebanon accusing Hezbollah of violating the truce, while progress is being made in talks with Iran.

title: Panic in Moscow: gas purchases and black rain after refinery attack
summary: The population of Moscow experienced scenes of panic with gas purchases and reports of black-colored rain after an attack on a refinery, reflecting growing instability.

title: US deploys missiles in Japan and trains island defenses amid Chinese pressure
summary: The United States will deploy intermediate-range missiles in Japan while conducting joint military exercises for the defense of remote islands, in response to growing Chinese threats against Taiwan.

title: Proposal to reduce consumption tax to 1% for two years sparks debate
summary: Popular Democratic Party leader Yuichiro Tamaki proposed lowering the consumption tax to 1% for two years, but experts warn about the fiscal impact and the possibility of a subsequent increase.

title: Japan strengthens artificial intelligence regulation in response to cyber risks
summary: The Japanese government presented a revision of the basic AI plan to strengthen cybersecurity and create specialized agencies for risk oversight.

title: China suspends sale of tour packages to Japan after brief resumption
summary: Chinese travel agencies stopped selling group tours to Japan after having resumed them, amid reports of pressure from the Chinese government.

title: China reduces exports of rare earth magnets to Japan by more than 30%
summary: Exports of rare earth magnets from China to Japan fell sharply in May, probably due to export restrictions that affect the technology supply chain.

title: Cuba approves market reforms but US considers them insufficient
summary: The Cuban government approved a set of economic reforms to liberalize the market, but the United States described them as late and superficial.

title: Brazil thrashes Haiti with Matheus Cunha brace and awakens in World Cup
summary: The Brazilian national team achieved its first World Cup victory by beating Haiti 3-0, with two goals from Cunha and one from Vinicius, showing improvement from its debut.

title: Paraguay prevails with one man down after new anti-discrimination rule
summary: Paraguay won 1-0 while playing with ten men for much of the match, after a player was sent off for covering his mouth in violation of the new anti-discrimination rule.

title: Eleven injured in Tokyo elementary school fire, evacuated through windows
summary: A fire on the third floor of a school in Tokyo's Kita ward left eleven injured, and teachers evacuated students through windows as smoke blocked the exits.

title: Pitcher Sumida Chihiro throws his second shutout of the season
summary: Sumida Chihiro threw a complete game shutout and struck out eight batters to lead the Seibu Lions to victory over the SoftBank Hawks.

title: Dominant Sasaki fails to get win despite brilliant start
summary: Sasaki Rōki allowed only one hit in five and one-third innings, but two consecutive home runs in the sixth inning denied him the decision; the Dodgers won in extra innings.

title: Yubari celebrates end of salary cuts 20 years after bankruptcy
summary: The city of Yubari, Hokkaido, achieved financial rehabilitation after two decades of severe adjustments, restoring full salaries for its municipal employees.

title: Japanese city plans to reintroduce tram 21 years after its elimination
summary: The prefectural governor is promoting a light rail transit (LRT) project in a city that abolished its tram in 2003, but the mayor is cautious about the cost and demand.
